---
title: "LRPHS03: Regularized-horseshoe ranking cross-check - letter-sound gain"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRPHS03 is the letter-sound companion to [`lrp-rli-hs-001`](../lrp-rli-hs-001/) (word-reading gain): an **independent Bayesian sensitivity check** on the gradient-boosting predictor ranking for letter-sound-knowledge gain (GB `lrp-rli-gbg-009`). The flagship code outcome previously had no horseshoe ranking cross-check — hs-001/002 cover word reading only. A regularized (Finnish) horseshoe sparse regression regresses the letter-sound post-count on its own T1 baseline plus the standardised T1 baselines of the other constructs, all sharing a global-local shrinkage prior; predictors are ranked by posterior `P(|beta| > delta)` and compared to the GB _cluster_ ranking at construct level.

_Ranking cross-check, NOT causal._ If the horseshoe order broadly agrees with the gradient-boosting order, that is reassurance the ranking is not an artefact of the tree method — it is not a causal claim. Every coefficient is a **latent-ability-confounded adjusted association**; the randomised causal effect for letter sounds lives in the ITT suite (`lrp-rli-itt-007`).

_Between-child framing, n ~ 54._ One row per child (T1 baselines → full-study letter-sound gain, `L_last | L_T1`), mirroring LRP65 / hs-001: the coefficients are between-child associations. At n ~ 54 with a shrinkage prior the intervals are wide by design; read the _ordering_, not the magnitudes. Hyperparameters are weakly-informative priors, not tuned.

## Model

Beta-Binomial letter-sound post-count conditioned on its own T1 baseline (`gamma_own`), with standardised T1 predictor baselines entering under a regularized-horseshoe prior (global scale `tau`, per-predictor local scale `lambda_k`, slab `c^2`):

$$
\eta_i = \alpha + \gamma_{\text{own}}\,\text{logit}_*(L_{\mathrm{pre},i}, N)
        + \sum_k \beta_k\, z_{k,i},
\qquad
\beta_k \sim \mathcal{N}\!\big(0,\ \tau\, \tilde{\lambda}_k\big).
$$

Construct predictor set: word reading `W`, receptive/expressive vocabulary `R`/`E`, blending `B`, basic concepts `F`, receptive grammar `T`, age, and the RLI-blocks / behaviour covariates — i.e. every non-floored measure baseline **other than the outcome** `L`. Floored / post-only measures (`P`, `N`) and the block-1 taught/not-taught vocabulary (unconfirmed denominators) are omitted.

![Model diagram](model_graph.png){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_results_horseshoe.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
